runs.root_cause: Vatican Museums CVR fell −0.63pp (4.47%→3.84%) because peak spring demand (+13% traffic) exceeded the Vatican's fixed timed-entry slot supply — shrinking available booking dates by 5.1 per 30-day window — while a secondary checkout failure on iOS (C2A) and Android (A2O) added a compounding −40% Shapley contribution.

S2C fell from 31.8% to 25.2% (−6.65pp, 70% of ΔCVR) because the post period captured the wind-down of Hawaii's spring break season: high-intent spring break visitors were replaced by lower-intent off-peak tourists. No supply, pricing, or UX change — purely a demand quality decline.
